title: goeppertia donnell-smithii
intro: Goeppertia donnell-smithii, also known as the False Calathea or Calathea donnell-smithii, is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae. Native to Mexico and Central America, it has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and skin irritations, offering benefits like reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it can also be used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
